other_observers: John Streeter (from Centennial, CO); Yes, I believe I am the original finder. Cole Wild reported seeing 1 of these birds at this location 3 days later.
specific_location: Just west (a few feet) of the intersection of Logan County Roads 95 and 68, there is a bridge over an inlet canal that flows north and east into Jumbo Reservoir. All three birds were in this canal, and at their furthest were right at the point where the canal bends eastward out of view from the bridge towards the reservoir.
description: All three birds appeared identical, in streaked brown plumage standing upright along the stream bank. The bills were very dark and quite thick and heavy, especially toward the base of the bills. The eyes were yellow and large. The necks seemed lengthy, and the heads were rounded. All three individuals showed very fine spots like little triangles at the tips of the wing coverts, along with clean white edging to those coverts.Behaviors: The first bird I saw was flushed from a location closer to the bridge I was on. It flew directly to a spot along the stream near a bend, right across from 2 other herons, perhaps siblings. They mostly stood still as I watched, occasionally stepping slowly along the water's edge. They looked intently on the water, and I witnessed on at least 2 occasions a bird jabbing into the water and pulling out a crayfish.Call: no sounds heard.Plumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: Eric Defonso, Eric DeFonso, Eric DeFonso
